title: Vajiralongkorn becomes King of Thailand
fact: Vajiralongkorn, also known by his regnal name Rama X, is King of Thailand, reigning since 2016.

title: Bhumibol Adulyadej becomes King of Thailand
fact: Bhumibol Adulyadej, titled Rama IX, was King of Thailand from 9 June 1946 until his death in 2016.

title: Chulalongkorn becomes King of Thailand
fact: Chulalongkorn, posthumously honoured as King Chulalongkorn the Great, was the fifth king of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, titled Rama V.

title: Mongkut becomes King of Thailand
fact: Mongkut, posthumously honoured as King Mongkut the Great, was the fourth king of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, titled Rama IV.

title: Ananda Mahidol becomes King of Thailand
fact: Ananda Mahidol was the eighth king of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, titled Rama VIII.

title: Phutthayotfa Chulalok becomes King of Thailand
fact: Phutthayotfa Chulalok, posthumously honoured as King Phutthayotfa Chulalok the Great, also known by his regnal name Rama I, was the founder of the Rattanakosin Kingdom and the first King of Siam from the reigning Chakri dynasty.

title: Prajadhipok becomes King of Thailand
fact: Prajadhipok, also known as Rama VII was the seventh monarch of the Chakri dynasty and the last king of Siam under the absolute monarchy.

title: Vajiravudh becomes King of Thailand
fact: Vajiravudh was the sixth king of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, titled Rama VI.

title: Taksin becomes King of Thailand
fact: Taksin the Great or the King of Thonburi was a Thai Chinese general who became the only King of Thonburi that ruled Siam from 1767 to 1782.

title: Naresuan becomes King of Thailand
fact: Naresuan, commonly known as Naresuan the Great, or Sanphet II was the 18th monarch of the Ayutthaya Kingdom and the 2nd of the Sukhothai dynasty.

title: Phutthaloetla Naphalai becomes King of Thailand
fact: Phutthaloetla Naphalai, also known by his regnal name Rama II, was the second King of Siam from the Chakri dynasty, ruling from 1809 to 1824.

title: Nangklao becomes King of Thailand
fact: Nangklao, also known by his posthumous regnal name Rama III, was King of Siam from 1824 to his death in 1851.

title: Narai becomes King of Thailand
fact: King Narai the Great, or Ramathibodi III, was the 27th monarch of Ayutthaya Kingdom, the 4th and last monarch of the Prasat Thong dynasty.

title: Ekkathat becomes King of Thailand
fact: Ekkathat or Borommoracha III or King of Suriyamarin Throne Hall was the 6th monarch of the Ban Phlu Luang dynasty, the 33rd and the last monarch of Ayutthaya Kingdom, ruling from 1758 to 7 April 1767, prior to the fall of Ayutthaya.

title: Sri Indraditya becomes King of Thailand
fact: Si Inthrathit was the first king of the Sukhothai Kingdom, a historical polity in what is now Thailand.
image: พ่อขุนศรีอินทราทิตย์_วัดคุ้งวารี_(cropped).jpg

title: Phetracha becomes King of Thailand
fact: Phetracha was the king of the Ayutthaya Kingdom from 1688 to 1703 and the founder of the Ban Phlu Luang dynasty, the final ruling house of Ayutthaya.

title: Suriyenthrathibodi becomes King of Thailand
fact: King Suriyenthrathibodi or Luang Sorasak —was originally named Duea or Maduea, and King Chulalongkorn later issued a royal judgement identifying his regnal name as Sanphet VIII.
image: พระบรมรูป_สมเด็จพระเจ้าเสือ_วัดไทร.jpg

title: Ram Khamhaeng becomes King of Thailand
fact: Ramkhamhaeng, commonly known as Pho Khun Ramkhamhaeng the Great, was the third king of the Phra Ruang dynasty, ruling the Sukhothai Kingdom—a historical polity in what is now Thailand—from 1279 to 1298, during its most prosperous era.

title: Ekathotsarot becomes King of Thailand
fact: Ekathotsarot or Sanphet III ; 1560 – 1610/11) was the King of Ayutthaya from 1605 to 1610/11 and overlord of Lan Na from 1605 to 1608/09, succeeding his brother Naresuan.

title: Uthumphon becomes King of Thailand
fact: Uthumphon Maha Thammarachathirat III or Uthumphon Mahaphon Phinit was the 32nd and penultimate monarch of the Ayutthaya Kingdom, ruling in 1758 for about three months.

title: Prasat Thong becomes King of Thailand
fact: Prasat Thong was the founder and first monarch of the Prasat Thong dynasty, the fourth ruling house of the Ayutthaya Kingdom.

title: Borommakot becomes King of Thailand
fact: King Borommakot or King Maha Thammarachathirat II was the king of Ayutthaya from 1733 to 1758.

title: Thai Sa becomes King of Thailand
fact: Prince Phet, later known as King Thai Sa and formally styled as the King of Banyongrattanat Palace, was the King of the Ayutthaya Kingdom from 1709 to 1733 and the third ruler of the Ban Phlu Luang dynasty.
